title: rhamnus cathartica subsp. hydriensis
intro: Rhamnus cathartica subsp. hydriensis, also known as the Greek buckthorn, is a subspecies of the common buckthorn plant (Rhamnus cathartica) native to Greece and other parts of the Mediterranean region. It is a deciduous shrub with medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which have laxative and purgative effects. The Greek buckthorn has been used to treat various health issues, including constipation, gallstones, and liver problems, thanks to its ability to stimulate digestive function and promote bile production. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties, and it has also been used in folk medicine to treat a range of other ailments.
